patch.value: pm history --stats / pm stats --storage: aggregate history-stream metrics
No command surfaces per-stream sizes, line counts, or depth distribution; essential for deciding when to compact/redact and for storage planning.
Outputs total streams, lines, bytes, top-N by size/depth, oldest/newest entries; json+human; reuses drift cache where fresh (no full re-verify).
No command surfaces per-stream sizes, line counts, or depth distribution; essential for deciding when to compact/redact and for storage planning.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Sub-agent starting: src/core/history/history-storage-stats.ts — pure aggregate of history streams (total streams/lines/bytes, top-N largest+deepest, global oldest/newest). Orchestrator wires into pm stats --storage.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text:
Sub-agent DONE: history-storage-stats.ts implemented and tested.

**Exported signature:**
- `HistoryStreamStat { id, bytes, lines, oldest_ts: string|null, newest_ts: string|null }`
- `HistoryStorageEntryRef { id, ts }`
- `HistoryStorageStats { total_streams, total_lines, total_bytes, largest_by_bytes, deepest_by_lines, oldest_entry, newest_entry }`
- `computeHistoryStorageStats(streams, options?): HistoryStorageStats`

**Behavior decisions:**
- topN defaults to 5; `Math.max(0, Math.floor(topN))` clamps negatives to 0 and floors fractional values. topN=0 returns empty arrays for both top-N fields.
- Unparseable JSON lines: counted toward `lines` total but ts is skipped for min/max tracking.
- Lines with missing or non-string `ts` field: counted as lines, skipped for ts.
- Invalid date strings (Date.parse → NaN): skipped for ts tracking.
- CRLF line endings supported via `/\r?\n/` split.
- Byte counting via `Buffer.byteLength(raw, 'utf8')` — correct for multibyte UTF-8.
- Tie-breaking: all top-N sorts and global oldest/newest use `id` ascending as the secondary key for determinism.
- Global oldest/newest: null when no stream has any parseable ts.

**Edge cases covered:** empty streams array, empty raw string stream, single stream, single-entry stream, stream with zero parseable ts (null oldest/newest), CRLF line endings, whitespace-only lines (not counted), topN default (>5 streams → 5 returned), topN=0 (empty arrays), topN>count (all returned), negative topN (clamped to 0), fractional topN (floored), multibyte UTF-8 byte counting (emoji + accented chars + Japanese), global ts tie-breaking by id.

**Test result:** 31 tests passed, 1 test file. Coverage: 100% statements (55/55), 100% branches (48/48), 100% functions (5/5), 100% lines (54/54).

**NOTE for orchestrator:** Project vitest.config.ts uses `include: ["tests/**/*.spec.ts"]` so the test file was created as `tests/unit/core/history/history-storage-stats.spec.ts` (not .test.ts). Verification command: `npx vitest run tests/unit/core/history/history-storage-stats.spec.ts`

patch.value: Add pm stats --storage: aggregate history-stream metrics

Shipped pm stats --storage via core/history/history-storage-stats.ts: total_streams/lines/bytes, largest_by_bytes + deepest_by_lines top-N, global oldest/newest entry. Reads streams once with no hash re-verification (lighter than the drift cache). The per-item 'pm history --stats' surface was folded into pm stats --storage since the aggregate belongs on stats.

Shipped pm stats --storage aggregate history-stream metrics (total_streams/lines/bytes, largest_by_bytes + deepest_by_lines top-N, global oldest/newest) via pure core/history/history-storage-stats.ts. 31 module tests (100%) + command test + manual e2e. Per-item 'pm history --stats' folded into pm stats --storage.
